and is reported to have commented on it. In B.S. 1.10 as
well as LI.7, the Acarya says that he has already written the Tdtrd.
Utpala invariably quotes from it and calls it Togaydtrd (under
XLIII.31). He explains that the author had already written B>J.,
Br. Tdtrd, and Br. Vivdhapapala. The author refers to it in many other
places too.8 B.J. mentions Vivdhapafala and Tdtrd as future works.4
Similarly in the Br. Tdtrd it is stated that Jdtaka has already
been written.5 Togaydtra IV.52 and Vivdhapatala 16, 97 speak of
Jdtaka as an earlier work. In his Vivrti on Togaydtrd Utpala gives the
order of Varaha's works as follows : (i) P. Siddhantika, (ii) Jataka,
(iii) Tak$yekamedhiya Tdtrd, (iv) Togaydtrd, (v) Vivdhapatala, and
(vi) Samhitd. But after writing all these works Varaha must have
1. The word Tikanika must be a vernacular name for 'small.' Cf. Kanna^a
word, cikani, in this sense.
2. Under B.J. XX. 10.
3. B. S. XUV. 14, 18; XLVII1. 22, 29.
4. B.J. XXIV. 16; XXVIII. 3.
